raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California government has appropriated $2.7 billion for wildfire and forest resilience.",
    "Assembly Bill 788 will provide policymakers and taxpayers with comprehensive data on state and federal investments in wildfire and forest resilience programs.",
    "AB 788 will direct the California Wildfire and Forest Resilience Task Force to create and maintain a comprehensive data portal of wildfire projects and expenditures.",
    "Piecemeal data collection and an inadequate reporting system has led to confusion regarding the status of current resilience programs.",
    "A 2022 study by the Pew Charitable Trusts found that states should explore opportunities to better track and share data on wildfire spending.",
    "Wildfire spending data should be made more accessible, transparent, and comprehensive across all levels of government to improve intergovernmental coordination.",
    "Increasing accountability and transparency in state and federal investments will ensure that taxpayer dollars are used effectively to reduce the main drivers of catastrophic wildfires."
  ]
}
```
